title: Java or Bedrock Edition?
description: PortalMod is being developed for **Minecraft: Java Edition**. Unfortunately, Bedrock Edition has a very different **add-on system** instead of mods. Besides the fact that supporting two completely different versions at the same time would be incredibly **time consuming**, such restrictions would also make it **impossible to realize** our vision in a complete way in that edition.

title: What mod loader does PortalMod use?
description: PortalMod runs on the **Forge mod loader**, as it's simply the leading mod loader for this version of the game. In the future, however, we will consider **various alternatives**.

title: Why 1.16.5?
description: The development of this mod started **back in 2021**, when this was the **latest version** available. Had we ported the (still being developed) mod every time a new Minecraft update released, it would have taken **a lot more time**. For this reason, we preferred to keep the **first release on 1.16.5**.

title: Will you port to modern versions?
description: Newer versions are obviously our **next big step** to achieve. However, we cannot give exact details at this moment.

title: Where can I report a bug / make a suggestion?
description: Simply create a new **issue** on our [GitHub repository](https://github.com/snowy-shack/PortalMod/issues).

title: Are there maps / generated test chambers included?
description: In the mod itself there are no maps or custom generated terrain, you have to be creative and **build it yourself**! However, if you want to play something pre-made, <#1367574911614451765> is full of **community made maps**.

title: Can I use a shader loader / graphics mod?
description: This mod was developed without taking those into account, mainly because in Forge 1.16.5 there **aren't stable and mature** graphics mods. Since having them compatible would need us to **remake most of the mod** from scratch, we preferred to keep it incompatible in this version of the game.

title: Can I use OptiFine?
description: For the same reason explained in the above answer, OptiFine was **not made compatible**. Furthermore, specifically OptiFine changes so much of Minecraft's code that it is **very hard for many mods** to be made compatible with it.

title: Why won't you add this feature?
description: One of the main reasons we are rejecting many suggestions is because we value the **quality of the mod** more than the **amount of features** we add. Our priority is making sure that the mod feels complete and that **everything has a purpose** without just being a gimmick. We focus on adding things to the game in such a way that they take into account all styles of playing, including survival too. Sadly for many, this philosophy also applies to things like **cores and GLaDOS**, which will not be included.

title: Will you add Portal 1 / Old Aperture styled testing elements?
description: We prefer to avoid adding **too many blocks** with the same function in order to keep all different items and blocks feel **fresh and unique**. This means we will not add testing elements with multiple appearances. However, we do already have many variants in regards to **building blocks**! Of course, you can also create or use a **resource pack** to change the looks of almost anything in the mod!

title: What blocks can I place Portals on?
description: The blocks portalable by default are: **Lunecast** and all its variations, **end stone**, and **concrete**. The portalability is also fully configurable, meaning a **datapack** can add to or override the default block tag located at `data/portalmod/tags/blocks/portal/portalable.json`. Furthermore, by setting the **game rule** `usePortalableBlacklist` to `true`, a blacklist located at `data/portalmod/tags/blocks/portal/unportalable.json` can be used instead. The default blacklist only has **Blackplate**, which means **all other blocks will be portalable**.

title: How do I use this block/item?
description: All items have a **tooltip** viewable holding **Ctrl (or Command)** in the inventory. Please refer to that before asking this type of questions.

title: How do I connect an Antline to a testing element?
description: Antlines cannot activate them directly. They need an **Antline Indicator** attached, which activates nearby testing elements, as well as showing the current state of the signal.

title: Why don't I see this Portal Gun skin in the Skin Selector?
description: If you own such skin, try using the **refresh button** at the bottom of the GUI. If that still didn't solve it, feel free to **ask us**.
